infobox.value: ([/ˈstjuːbən/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*STEW-bən*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key") or [/stjuːˈbɛn/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*stew-BEN*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"))[[3]](#cite_note-3) is a [county](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") located in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"). As of the [2020 census](/wiki/United_States_Census_2020 "United States Census 2020"), its population was 93,584.[[4]](#cite_note-QF-4) Its [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") is [Bath](/wiki/Bath_(village),_New_York "Bath (village), New York").[[5]](#cite_note-GR6-5) Its name is in honor of [Baron von Steuben](/wiki/Baron_von_Steuben "Baron von Steuben"), a [Prussian](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Prussia "Kingdom of Prussia") general who fought on the American side in the [American Revolutionary War](/wiki/American_Revolutionary_War "American Revolutionary War"), though it is not pronounced the same (German: [[fɔn ˈʃtɔʏbn̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German")). The county is part of the [Southern Tier](/wiki/Southern_Tier "Southern Tier") region of the state.

**Steuben County** ([/ˈstjuːbən/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*STEW-bən*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key") or [/stjuːˈbɛn/](/wiki/Help:IPA/English "Help:IPA/English") [*stew-BEN*](/wiki/Help:Pronunciation_respelling_key "Help:Pronunciation respelling key"))[[3]](#cite_note-3) is a [county](/wiki/County_(United_States) "County (United States)") located in the [U.S. state](/wiki/U.S._state "U.S. state") of [New York](/wiki/New_York_(state) "New York (state)"). As of the [2020 census](/wiki/United_States_Census_2020 "United States Census 2020"), its population was 93,584.[[4]](#cite_note-QF-4) Its [county seat](/wiki/County_seat "County seat") is [Bath](/wiki/Bath_(village),_New_York "Bath (village), New York").[[5]](#cite_note-GR6-5) Its name is in honor of [Baron von Steuben](/wiki/Baron_von_Steuben "Baron von Steuben"), a [Prussian](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Prussia "Kingdom of Prussia") general who fought on the American side in the [American Revolutionary War](/wiki/American_Revolutionary_War "American Revolutionary War"), though it is not pronounced the same (German: [[fɔn ˈʃtɔʏbn̩]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German")). The county is part of the [Southern Tier](/wiki/Southern_Tier "Southern Tier") region of the state.

Steuben County comprises the [Corning](/wiki/Corning_(city),_New_York "Corning (city), New York"), NY [micropolitan statistical area](/wiki/Micropolitan_statistical_area "Micropolitan statistical area"), which is also included in the [Elmira](/wiki/Elmira,_New_York "Elmira, New York")-Corning, NY [combined statistical area](/wiki/Combined_statistical_area "Combined statistical area").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steuben_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

See also: [Canisteo, New York](/wiki/Canisteo,_New_York "Canisteo, New York"); [Canisteo (village), New York](/wiki/Canisteo_(village),_New_York "Canisteo (village), New York"); [Hornell, New York](/wiki/Hornell,_New_York "Hornell, New York"); and [Greenwood, New York, insurrection of 1882](/wiki/Greenwood,_New_York,_insurrection_of_1882 "Greenwood, New York, insurrection of 1882")

[Ontario County](/wiki/Ontario_County,_New_York "Ontario County, New York") was established in 1789 to govern lands the state of New York had acquired in the [Phelps and Gorham Purchase](/wiki/Phelps_and_Gorham_Purchase "Phelps and Gorham Purchase"); at the time, it covered the entirety of [Western New York](/wiki/Western_New_York "Western New York"). Steuben County, much larger than today, was split off from Ontario County on March 8, 1796. In 1823, a portion of Steuben County was combined with a portion of Ontario County to form [Yates County](/wiki/Yates_County,_New_York "Yates County, New York"). Steuben County was further reduced in size on April 17, 1854, when a portion was combined with portions of [Chemung](/wiki/Chemung_County,_New_York "Chemung County, New York") and [Tompkins](/wiki/Tompkins_County,_New_York "Tompkins County, New York") Counties to form [Schuyler County](/wiki/Schuyler_County,_New_York "Schuyler County, New York").

In its earliest years, Steuben County was demographically and geographically linked to the [Susquehanna River](/wiki/Susquehanna_River "Susquehanna River") basin in Pennsylvania, leading to the port of [Baltimore](/wiki/Baltimore "Baltimore"). The Canisteo River, navigable as far as [Arkport](/wiki/Arkport "Arkport"), emptied into the [Chemung River](/wiki/Chemung_River "Chemung River") and it into the Susquehanna. No natural barriers, such as the [Cohoes Falls](/wiki/Cohoes_Falls "Cohoes Falls") on the Mohawk, existed to impede transportation, and timber and other agricultural products were easily shipped downriver from what are the towns of [Addison](/wiki/Addison,_New_York "Addison, New York"), [Canisteo](/wiki/Canisteo,_New_York "Canisteo, New York"), and [Hornellsville](/wiki/Hornellsville,_New_York "Hornellsville, New York"). Prior to the opening of the [Erie Canal](/wiki/Erie_Canal "Erie Canal") in 1825, followed by the railroads, communication with the Hudson Valley and New York City was difficult. Limited to horses, mules, and donkeys, using it to ship bulky agricultural products was far too expensive.

In 1892, a bill was introduced in the legislature to split Steuben County, with Canisteo, Corning, and "the south towns" becoming Lincoln County. It did not pass.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steuben_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f5/Steuben_County_Courthouse%2C_Corning%2C_New_York.jpg/250px-Steuben_County_Courthouse%2C_Corning%2C_New_York.jpg)](/wiki/File:Steuben_County_Courthouse,_Corning,_New_York.jpg)

Former Steuben County Courthouse in [Corning](/wiki/Corning,_New_York "Corning, New York")

According to the [U.S. Census Bureau](/wiki/U.S._Census_Bureau "U.S. Census Bureau"), the county has a total area of 1,404 square miles (3,640 km2), of which 14 square miles (36 km2) (1.0%) are covered by water.[[7]](#cite_note-GR1-7)

Steuben County is in the southwestern part of New York, immediately north of the Pennsylvania border. The population of Steuben County according to the 2000 [U. S.](/wiki/United_States "United States") [census](/wiki/Census "Census") was 98,726. The county is in the [Southern Tier](/wiki/Southern_Tier "Southern Tier") region of New York.

## Government and politics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steuben_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Government and politics")]

Steuben County is governed by a 17-member legislature headed by a chairman.

### State and federal government

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steuben_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: State and federal government")]

Steuben County is a Republican stronghold in national elections. The last Democrat to carry the county was [Lyndon Johnson](/wiki/Lyndon_B._Johnson "Lyndon B. Johnson") in his 1964 landslide.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

### 2000 census

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steuben_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: 2000 census")]

As of the [census](/wiki/Census "Census")[[28]](#cite_note-GR2-28) of 2000, 98,726 people, 39,071 households, and 26,216 families were residing in the county. The [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") was 71 people per square mile (27 people/km2). The 46,132 housing units had an average density of 33 units per square mile (13/km2). The [racial makeup](/wiki/Race_(United_States_Census) "Race (United States Census)") of the county was 96.43% White, 1.36% African American, 0.27% Native American, 0.90% Asian, 0.02% Pacific Islander, 0.21% from other races, and 0.81% from two or more races. [Hispanic](/wiki/Hispanic_(U.S._Census) "Hispanic (U.S. Census)") or [Latino](/wiki/Latino_(U.S._Census) "Latino (U.S. Census)") of any race were 0.81% of the population. About 18.6% were of German, 15.2% English, 14.4% American, 13.6% Irish, and 8.3% Italian ancestry according to [Census 2000](/wiki/Census_2000 "Census 2000"); 96.5% spoke English and 1.3% Spanish as their first language.

Of the 39,071 households, 31.8% had children under 18 living with them, 51.7% were married couples living together, 10.6% had a female householder with no husband present, and 32.9% were not families. About 27.2% of all households were made up of individuals, and 11.9% had someone living alone who was 65 or older. The average household size was 2.49 and the average family size was 3.01.

In the county, the age distribution was 26.0% under 18, 7.40 from 18 to 24, 27.2% from 25 to 44, 24.2% from 45 to 64, and 15.2% who were 65 or older. The median age was 38 years. For every 100 females, there were 96.0 males. For every 100 females 18 and over, there were 93.2 males.

The median income for a household in the county was $35,479 and for a family was $41,940. Males had a median income of $32,155 versus $24,163 for females. The [per capita income](/wiki/Per_capita_income "Per capita income") for the county was $18,197. About 9.9% of families and 13.2% of the population were below the [poverty line](/wiki/Poverty_line "Poverty line"), including 18.7% of those under 18 and 5.8% of those 65 or over.

## Industry

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Steuben_County,_New_York&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Industry")]

The largest employer in Steuben County is [Corning, Inc.](/wiki/Corning,_Inc. "Corning, Inc.") (formerly Corning Glass Works), the world headquarters of a large firm (34,000 employed worldwide), which manufactures specialty glass and related products. Related is the nearby [Corning Museum of Glass](/wiki/Corning_Museum_of_Glass "Corning Museum of Glass"). A wine industry is in [Hammondsport](/wiki/Hammondsport,_New_York "Hammondsport, New York"), also the headquarters of the [Mercury Corporation](/wiki/Mercury_Corporation "Mercury Corporation"), a custom manufacturer, formerly of aircraft and aircraft components, and a museum of aviation, the [Glenn H. Curtiss Museum](/wiki/Glenn_H._Curtiss_Museum "Glenn H. Curtiss Museum"), is in Hammondsport. Former industries in Steuben County are the [Steuben Glass Works](/wiki/Steuben_Glass_Works "Steuben Glass Works"), in Corning, now part of Corning Glass Works, and the [Erie Railroad](/wiki/Erie_Railroad "Erie Railroad") repair shops, in [Hornell](/wiki/Hornell,_New_York "Hornell, New York").
